Major policy update reshapes trade routes

The federal government announced a broad policy package this morning that changes how critical goods move across the country while balancing climate and affordability concerns. Officials said the plan aims to reduce delays, improve transparency, and coordinate with provinces over the next twelve months.

Analysts expect immediate debate in parliament, where opposition parties are requesting committee hearings and independent economic modeling. Residents in affected regions said they support stronger oversight but want clear timelines and practical cost protections.
